evidenceTypesSupplied: Local derived ACTN3 and ACE fitness-trait evidence.
Public Gene-In-Form fitness sample PDF rows used as observed output-format examples.
Consumer education references for athletic-performance complexity, polygenic limits, and source binding.
sectionsUnavailable: No calibrated fitness trait, training-response, injury-risk, recovery, VO2 max, or sport-suitability model is supplied with this fixture.
No personal workout, supplement, nutrition, injury-prevention, recovery, medical-clearance, VO2max, or sport-selection recommendation is reported.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACTN3 rs1815739 CC call is available. This is educational trait context only and does not predict strength, speed, endurance, training response, injury risk, recovery, or athletic ability.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACTN3 is discussed in athletic-performance research, but a single marker cannot personalize a workout, sport choice, injury-prevention plan, or performance expectation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACE DD context row is available. This is educational trait context only and is not enough to choose sports, prescribe workouts, estimate VO2 max, or predict training response.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACE is discussed in athletic-performance research, but training history, health, sleep, nutrition, environment, opportunity, and many other factors remain important.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated fitness trait score, training-response model, injury-risk model, recovery model, VO2 max model, or ancestry applicability metadata is supplied.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NHGRI polygenic risk scores / GWAS Catalog
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The local output can describe supplied marker context and unavailable sections, but it must not quantify performance, injury, recovery, sport choice, or training response without a validated model.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: SOD2 common AG, sample AA; impact: slow recovery rate.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 6 of the public Gene-In-Form sample PDF; this is observed sample output, not a local recovery prediction or training recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: NRF2 common AA, sample AC; impact: normal response to training.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: GDF5 common AA, sample GG; COL5A1 common CT, sample CT; impact: reduced risk for Achilles' Tendon Pathologies.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: COL5A1 common CT, sample CT; impact: raw genetic data file missing results for this gene; injury summary labels ACL average risk.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 5-6 of the public Gene-In-Form sample PDF; this is observed sample output, not a local ACL injury-risk or prevention conclusion.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: GDF5 common TT, sample CC; impact: reduced risk for osteoarthritis.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 6 of the public Gene-In-Form sample PDF; this is observed sample output, not a local osteoarthritis, injury-risk, or medical conclusion.

fitnessContext: Use this report as educational fitness genetics context, not as a workout, supplement, or nutrition plan.
Treat Gene-In-Form sample training, recovery, VO2max, injury, and sport-focus language as observed sample-report structure unless validated local evidence supports equivalent sections.
Plan training, injury prevention, recovery, and performance goals with qualified coaches, clinicians, or other appropriate professionals.
Do not use genotype alone for sport selection, supplement decisions, medical exercise clearance, nutrition planning, or expectations about athletic ability.

plainEnglishLimits: Fitness and training response depend on genetics, training history, health, sleep, nutrition, environment, opportunity, and professional review, not genotype alone.
Public sample rows show observed report format and source binding; they are not local personal findings unless the local run supplies equivalent validated evidence.
The local fixture does not include training history, injury history, clinical status, exercise testing, nutrition history, coach assessment, or a calibrated fitness model.

references.title: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores

missingInputs: No calibrated fitness performance, training-response, injury-risk, recovery, VO2 max, or sport-suitability model is supplied.
No training history, injury history, sleep, nutrition, medical history, fitness testing, coach assessment, clinician assessment, or dietitian assessment is supplied.
Sample-person genotypes differ from the local fixture and are kept as observed sample output examples only.
uncertainty: Fitness, training response, injury risk, recovery, and sport outcomes are complex traits influenced by genetics plus non-genetic context and professional review.
Individual sample-report markers and fitness rows should not be treated as deterministic local performance, injury, recovery, or sport-selection predictions.
Public sample rows are examples from a sample report and may not represent the local user.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not predict elite ability, prescribe workouts, recommend sport selection, estimate injury risk, estimate recovery, quantify VO2 max, recommend supplements, create nutrition plans, or provide medical exercise clearance.
The Gene-In-Form public sample PDF is source-bound, and authenticated marketplace detail-page reportData is captured for product metadata and detail-page boundaries.
